articles.title: GTA 6 physical edition is code in a box, will have early release date for preloading
articles.excerpt: Rockstar Games announced that the physical version of Grand Theft Auto 6 won't come on disc, hinting at a large file size
articles.body: Rockstar Games has announced that physical copies of Grand Theft Auto 6 won't come on disc. They will "contain a download code inside the box," according to the developer's announcement of pre-order details, including the game's price and Ultimate Edition and pre-order bonuses .

articles.title: Critical Role has considered doing a Marvel-style What If...? series
articles.excerpt: Critical Role's Travis Willingham and Laura Bailey reveal they've discussed creating a Marvel-style What If... series based on the D&D actual play.
articles.body: The founders of Critical Role have never been shy about expressing how many ideas and projects they have to expand the worlds they've created. Recently, Critical Role's CEO, Travis Willingham, said that there was still a lot to explore in the world of Exandria and, more importantly, that Critical Role's venture into new and exciting projects wasn't limited to animated adaptations but also included video games and books.

articles.title: It's a crime these Lego Star Wars sets are being retired but you can save big before they go
articles.excerpt: I love Lego Star Wars but I think it should be illegal to retire sets, especially with the Star Wars 50th anniversary next year. Luckily, there are big savings on these retired and retiring models.
articles.body: Lego is amazing, and Lego Star Wars in particular. The fact that you can spend $1000 on a gigantic Death Star set is a clear sign it's not just for kids. But there's a catch; Lego retires sets, meaning that, unless you're willing to pay silly prices on eBay, they're forever out of your reach. Oh, sure, Lego might need space for new sets, and stores likewise, but that's no excuse to rob builders of the best Lego Star Wars sets , especially with Star Wars' 50th anniversary next year. I've rounded up the best deals on retired or retiring Lego sets. Some of these sets have been this price before Prime Day but don't count on them sticking around much longer. Save $24 on this Lego model of the first ship you see in Star Wars, pursued by a Star Destroyer right after the opening crawl.

articles.title: How I Built Pathway AI: A Full-Stack SaaS Platform in 1 Month
articles.excerpt: How I Built Pathway AI: A Full-Stack SaaS Platform in 1 Month I built Pathway AI, a SaaS platform for international students planning their study abroad journey, in roughly one month. Here's how I wen…
articles.body: How I Built Pathway AI: A Full-Stack SaaS Platform in 1 Month I built Pathway AI , a SaaS platform for international students planning their study abroad journey, in roughly one month. Here's how I went from concept to live product using Next.js, Supabase, and TypeScript. The Problem International students face overwhelming challenges when planning to study abroad: fragmented information, biased agents, unclear visa requirements, and no clear way to compare destinations. I wanted to build something that solved this. The Stack Frontend: Next.js (App Router) + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS Backend: Supabase (PostgreSQL) + Edge Functions AI: Gemini API Deployment: Vercel Monitoring: Sentry for error tracking Rate Limiting: Upstash Redis Architecture Overview 1.

articles.title: Clearbit Shut Down Its Free Tier — The Honest Alternatives in 2026
articles.excerpt: If you came here because your Clearbit enrichment broke, you're not imagining it. HubSpot acquired Clearbit and folded it into Breeze Intelligence. The standalone Clearbit Enrichment API, the free tie…
articles.body: If you came here because your Clearbit enrichment broke, you're not imagining it. HubSpot acquired Clearbit and folded it into Breeze Intelligence . The standalone Clearbit Enrichment API, the free tier, and the old logo/reveal endpoints have been sunset or pulled into the HubSpot ecosystem. If you were calling Clearbit as a plain API — outside HubSpot — you've effectively been orphaned. This post is a straight, no-hype map of where to go next. I build one of the alternatives (more on that below, clearly flagged), so I'll be upfront about where my tool fits and where it doesn't — because picking the wrong category wastes weeks. First, decide what you actually need Most "Clearbit alternative" lists lump everything together. Don't.

The film led to an excellent spinoff series focused on the Penguin and rumors abound about who Reeves will pick to terrorize Gotham in The Batman Part 2 . But the Riddler is back in the spotlight in a trailer for Reeves’ next project.

articles.title: Common Google Indexing Issues (And How Developers Can Fix Them)
articles.excerpt: You publish a new page. The content looks great. The design is polished. The page is live. A few days later, you search Google expecting to see it ranking... and nothing appears. If you've opened Goog…
articles.body: You publish a new page. The content looks great. The design is polished. The page is live. A few days later, you search Google expecting to see it ranking... and nothing appears. If you've opened Google Search Console and seen messages like: Discovered – currently not indexed Crawled – currently not indexed Excluded by 'noindex' tag Duplicate without user-selected canonical You're not alone and the culprit is almost always a technical issue, not content quality. Why Indexing Matters Before a page can rank, Google must complete four steps: Discover it Crawl it Understand it Index it If any step fails, your page won't appear in search results — regardless of how good the content is. Let's walk through the most common places this breaks down. 1.

articles.title: When the AI Prompts You: Reversing the Relationship in Experimental Interfaces
articles.excerpt: You open an app. You expect to type a question. Instead, the app asks you one. "What are you afraid of?" You hesitate. You type: "Failure." The app asks: "Why is failure so frightening to you?" You ty…
articles.body: You open an app. You expect to type a question. Instead, the app asks you one. "What are you afraid of?" You hesitate. You type: "Failure." The app asks: "Why is failure so frightening to you?" You type: "Because it means I'm not enough." The app asks: "Not enough for whom?" You pause. You are not sure if this is a therapy bot or a social experiment. You are not sure if you want to continue. You are not used to being the respondent. This is the reversed interface. The AI does not wait for your prompt. It prompts you. It asks questions. It probes your beliefs, your logic, your hidden assumptions. The relationship is flipped. You are no longer the prompter. You are the promptee. The Architecture of the Reverse Prompt The reversed interface is not a chatbot. It is an interrogator.

articles.title: OnePlus could be reduced to an OPPO product line under alleged new plans
articles.excerpt: A new report on the heels of realme India CEO's resignation spells out doom for OnePlus' independence.
articles.body: Credit: Ryan Haines / Android Authority TL;DR realme India CEO Mr. Michael Guo has stepped down due to health reasons, though a report suggests the resignation came amid a sweeping restructuring exercise to integrate realme with OnePlus under OPPO’s broader parent structure. OPPO’s long-term plans reportedly aim to bring OPPO, realme, and OnePlus under a single operating structure, managing them as distinct product series rather than independent brands. The effect of this move on OnePlus’ global operations is unknown, but it follows extensive staff departures across OnePlus Europe and the disappearance of display units in North America.

articles.title: 'Everybody wanted to be the first': Apollo astronauts were more competitive, Artemis 2 pilot says
articles.excerpt: NASA's Artemis moon program differs from Apollo in many ways, including the attitudes of its astronauts.
articles.body: A lot has changed since NASA last sent astronauts to the moon — including the attitudes of those space explorers. That's the view of Victor Glover , the NASA astronaut who served as pilot on the Artemis 2 mission around the moon's far side this past April. "When you look back on the Apollo missions, there was a lot more competition back in the office. Everybody wanted to be the first, and then everybody wanted to be the next," Glover told Angels Broadcast Television on June 12, shortly before he threw out the first pitch before a baseball game between the Los Angeles Angels and the Tampa Bay Rays. "I think our office learned a lot from them," he added. "There are some good things about that. It makes you work really hard, but it also can create some unnecessary conflict.

articles.title: Stripe, Anthropic and OpenAI are backing an effort to stop respiratory infections
articles.excerpt: The common cold comes for us all—often more than once a year. And there is no way to prevent it. The best you can do is take vitamin C and stay away from people with the sniffles. Now, the payment com…
articles.body: The common cold comes for us all—often more than once a year. And there is no way to prevent it. The best you can do is take vitamin C and stay away from people with the sniffles. Now, the payment company Stripe, founded by brothers Patrick and John Collison, says it will fund a new $500-million nonprofit whose goal is preventing both the common cold and the flu. Its eventual aim is to get rid of respiratory viruses altogether. The new organization, called Intercept, will use grants and investments to back prevention approaches, including vaccines, as well as large-scale air-cleaning systems for schools, offices, and other public spaces.

articles.title: Hubble Space Telescope images galaxy scientists thought was impossible to find
articles.excerpt: A faraway galaxy has been caught blowing away the cosmic fog of hydrogen that filled the universe once upon a time.
articles.body: A bright, dense cluster of hot, massive stars in a galaxy that existed 1.4 billion years after the big bang has been found helping to end the early universe's foggy days during which neutral hydrogen gas was draped across the cosmos, obscuring ultraviolet light from luminous objects. The cluster was found emitting ultraviolet light in a small but quickly growing galaxy by the Hubble Space Telescope . The presence of this ultraviolet light, and the star -forming history of the cluster producing it, suggests that bursts of star formation contributed to waves of ionizing radiation that gradually cleared out the opaque neutral hydrogen. In the aftermath of the big bang , the universe was filled with neutral hydrogen gas that is opaque at short wavelengths of light, such as ultraviolet.
articles.author: Keith Cooper

articles.title: White House drastically shortens deadline for dropping quantum-vulnerable crypto
articles.excerpt: Order warns of national security risks if post-quantum cryptography isn't adopted in time.
articles.body: The White House is drastically shortening the deadline for government agencies and organizations to adopt new quantum-resistant encryption systems that will withstand attacks that use quantum computers, as the federal government seeks to protect decades’ worth of secrets belonging to militaries, banks, governments, and most individuals on Earth. The executive order, titled Securing the Nation against Advanced Cryptographic Attacks , requires computing systems for “high-value assets” and “high-impact systems” to transition to post-quantum cryptographic key establishment schemes by December 31, 2030, and to quantum-safe digital signature schemes by December 31, 2031.
